articles.title: Heatwave alert issued for four Himachal districts - The Times of India
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Shimla: The meteorological department has issued a yellow warning for heatwave to severe heatwave conditions at isolated places in Una, Kangra, Mandi and Kullu districts on April 26.
On Saturday, the highest maximum temperature of 41.4°C was recorded in Una, while the lowest minimum temperature of 6.1° was reported at Kukumseri in Lahaul and Spiti district. The department has forecast no significant change in minimum and maximum temperatures across most parts of the state over the next two to three days.
Meanwhile, thunderstorms, accompanied by lightning and gusty winds (30-40 km per hour), are likely at isolated places in Chamba, Kangra, Mandi, Kullu, Shimla and Sirmaur districts on April 28 and 29.
Light rain is expected at isolated places in the state on April 27 and May 1, with light to moderate rainfall likely between April 28 and 30. Dry weather is expected to prevail during the remaining days of next week.
